a_image.alt: Minimalism style mood board — Design Philosophy · Less Is More

sections.a_eyebrow: Flat Design · Minimal · Whitespace · Clean UI · Professional
sections.a_description: The original design philosophy that launched a thousand frameworks — Minimalism is the disciplined art of knowing what to leave out. No decorative elements, no gratuitous animations, no visual noise. Just carefully chosen typography, purposeful whitespace, and the confidence to let content stand on its own without visual crutches.

This foundational style influences virtually every modern design system, portfolio template, and startup landing page. You'll see its principles on architectural firm websites, design studio portfolios, premium product pages, and any interface that follows Dieter Rams' principle that good design is as little design as possible — the aesthetic philosophy that proves emptiness can be the most powerful design element.

The foundational, less-is-more aesthetic of minimalist web design — maximum impact through maximum reduction, where every element earns its place on the page. Background: pure white (#ffffff). The .inner panel has a clean white background (#ffffff) with a single thin top border (1px solid #e5e5e5) creating minimal section separation — just enough structure, nothing more. Colors: deep charcoal (#1a1a1a) as the sole accent — hierarchy through typography weight and size, not color. Near-black (#1a1a1a) for headings, medium gray (#737373) for body text. The aesthetic is principled reduction — not empty, but intentional, every element placed with purpose. Set in Suisse Intl — the Swiss-designed grotesque that embodies minimalist design principles, neutral yet characterful. Heading at weight 500, 26px, line-height 1.3, letter-spacing -0.3px, color #1a1a1a. Eyebrow at weight 400, 11px, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #737373. Body at weight 400, 15px, line-height 1.7, color #737373. Buttons: primary — charcoal (#1a1a1a), white text, 4px border-radius. Secondary: transparent, charcoal border. Alternatives: Aktiv Grotesk (clean Swiss), Untitled Sans (minimal design standard).
{What this palette represents — the core identity}
